user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image clearly shows the rear of the vehicle with significant damage to the rear bumper and trunk area, matching the claimed object and part.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image clearly shows visible damage (dents, deformation, and detachment) on the rear bumper and trunk area, consistent with the user's claim of new damage found overnight.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 clearly shows the front bumper with visible scratches and scuff marks consistent with the user's claim. Image 2 shows the full front of the vehicle for identity context.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 provides clear visual evidence of scratches on the front bumper, matching the user's description of 'front bumper par scratch'.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 clearly shows the windshield with significant cracking consistent with the user's description of a stone impact. Image 2 provides context of the vehicle front.
claim_status_justification: Visual evidence in img_1 confirms a crack on the windshield matching the user's claim of stone impact damage.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the side mirror with visible damage (cracks/spiderweb pattern) matching the customer's claim of the mirror being damaged and not sitting right.
claim_status_justification: Image evidence confirms the claimed damage to the side mirror. The mirror glass shows significant cracking consistent with a clip impact.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Images clearly show the rear of the vehicle and the rear bumper area. img_1 provides a close-up view of a dent on the rear bumper, and img_2 shows the full rear context including the bumper and taillights.
claim_status_justification: Visual evidence in img_1 clearly shows a dent on the rear bumper consistent with the user's claim of being tapped from behind. img_2 confirms the location as the rear of the vehicle.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image (img_1) shows the side profile of the car, including the side mirror and door, but does not clearly show the front headlight area claimed by the user. The headlight is not visible in the frame.
claim_status_justification: The user claims a headlight crack, but the provided image only shows the side of the vehicle. The front headlight area is not visible, making it impossible to verify the claimed damage.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 2 clearly shows a dent on the car door panel, matching the user's claim. Image 1 provides context but is blurry.
claim_status_justification: Image 2 provides clear visual evidence of a dent on the door panel, consistent with the user's description.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image shows a severely damaged vehicle with extensive front-end destruction, fire damage, and missing components. This does not match the claimed condition of a 'scratch across the top panel' on the hood after service. The image appears to be a stock photo (watermarks visible) and depicts catastrophic damage inconsistent with the claim.
claim_status_justification: The image shows catastrophic front-end damage and fire, not a minor scratch on the hood. The image is also a stock photo with watermarks, making it invalid for this specific claim.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the laptop screen with extensive cracking, matching the user's claim of a cracked display glass after falling.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly displays a cracked laptop screen consistent with the user's report of the display glass cracking after falling.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Images clearly show the laptop hinge area with visible damage consistent with the claim of a broken hinge and wobbling screen.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 provides a close-up view of the hinge area showing structural damage and separation consistent with the customer's description of a broken hinge and screen wobble. Image 2 shows the overall laptop state.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image clearly shows water droplets and liquid pooling on the laptop keyboard, matching the claimed water spill and stain.
claim_status_justification: Visual evidence of water on the keyboard directly supports the customer's claim of spilling water near the laptop affecting the keys.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Images clearly show the laptop and the claimed damage. img_1 shows the overall laptop, img_2 shows a close-up of the dent on the corner/lid.
claim_status_justification: The submitted images clearly show a dent on the corner of the laptop lid, consistent with the user's claim that the laptop bag hit the floor causing a dent.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the laptop screen with visible cracks radiating from a central impact point, matching the user's claim of screen damage.
claim_status_justification: Visual evidence confirms the claimed screen damage (glass shatter) on the laptop. The image clearly displays the cracked screen area described by the user.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image clearly shows the laptop trackpad area with a visible white circle highlighting a specific spot on the surface, consistent with the claimed physical damage around the trackpad.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image clearly displays the trackpad area with a visible mark or scratch highlighted by a white annotation, supporting the customer's claim of physical damage in that specific region.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the exterior of a cardboard package with a visible crushed corner and tape damage, matching the customer's claim.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image (img_1) clearly displays a crushed corner on the package exterior, consistent with the customer's report of a crushed corner.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 clearly shows the package exterior with significant tearing on the side and seal area, matching the customer's description of 'seal wali side phati hui thi' (seal side torn). Image 2 shows the intact package for comparison or context, but Image 1 provides the necessary visual evidence of the damage.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image (img_1) clearly depicts a torn cardboard box with damaged tape/seal on the side, consistent with the user's claim of the package appearing opened or having a torn seal. The damage is visible and relevant to the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image clearly shows the package exterior with visible water stains and corner damage.
claim_status_justification: Visual evidence confirms water staining on the package exterior as claimed by the user.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 clearly shows the opened package interior filled with packing material, allowing visual assessment of the contents area. Image 2 shows the exterior seal and label.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 clearly shows the package interior is filled only with packing material (newspaper/paper) and no product item is visible, supporting the claim that the item is missing.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image shows a dented food can, not a shipping box. The claim is about a crushed shipping box exterior, but the image displays a product item (can) with a dent, which is a different object and damage type than claimed.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a dented food can, not a shipping box. The claim is about a crushed shipping box exterior, but the image displays a product item (can) with a dent, which is a different object and damage type than claimed.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 clearly shows a torn seal on the package box with visible damage to the tamper-evident tape. Image 2 shows a different package with intact seals, but Image 1 provides sufficient visual evidence of the claimed condition.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image (img_1) clearly displays a torn seal on the package box, matching the customer's claim of a torn-open package. The damage is visible and relevant to the claim.
